svg.aria-label: Three-channel gate conditioning perfboard placement map on a square 2.54 pitch grid
svg.text: Three gate channels on one square 2.54-style perfboard build. One SN74LVC14AN DIP-14 handles GATE0, GATE1, and GATE2 with the first three inverter sections; the remaining inputs must be tied to a defined level.
Main placement on square pad grid

Top rail is 3V3 on row A. Bottom rail is GND on row L. Each channel uses a 10k series resistor, a 100k pull-down, a 1N4148 clamp pair, and one inverter section inside U2. The three outputs go to BCM16, BCM19, and BCM20.

g.text: Unused SN74LVC14AN inputs must not float.
Tie pins 9, 11, and 13 to GND or 3V3.
Leave the matching outputs 8, 10, and 12 unconnected.
This map uses only channels 1, 2, and 3.

svg.text: This is the full 3-channel gate board, not a one-channel repeat block.
Use U2 pin pairs 1/2, 3/4, and 5/6 for GATE0, GATE1, and GATE2.
Row A carries 3V3 for U2 and the upper clamp diodes. Row L carries GND, the pull-down returns, and the jack sleeves.
Each jack passes through 10k before the node reaches the clamp pair or the IC input.
Each 100k pull-down holds the input low when the patch cable is absent or the source is open.
Coordinates below follow the board above. J2, J3, and J4 are off-board panel jacks. The perfboard carries the resistors, clamp diodes, the SN74LVC14AN, and the GPIO header connections.
Use three mono jacks. Tips land at B1, D1, and F1. All sleeves return to the shared ground rail on row L.
These are the first parts after the jacks. Keep them near the left edge so cable transients hit the resistor before anything else.
All three pull-downs share the same lower rail. That is fine because the gate channels only meet again at ground.
Use six 1N4148 diodes total. For each channel, one diode clamps upward to 3V3 and one clamps downward to GND.
Use pin pairs 1/2, 3/4, and 5/6. Pin 14 goes to 3V3 and pin 7 goes to GND. Tie the spare inputs 9, 11, and 13 to a fixed level.
Route the three signal wires off-board to the Pi header or the Waveshare header adapter, with one shared ground return from row L.
Net summary: GATE0 uses pins 1 and 2, GATE1 uses pins 3 and 4, GATE2 uses pins 5 and 6. All three channels share U2 pin 14 for 3V3 and pin 7 for GND.
